name: arc validation allows actually inside abstract latent answer axis

source.purpose: Regression for a live Forty Thousand arc where a valid impact axis was rejected because it used the word actually.

arcPlan.title: Forty Thousand: Black-Channel Run

sourceHook.title: Forty Thousand
sourceHook.premise: A forty-thousand-credit lien creates a back-channel courier offer.
sourceHook.crucible: Freedom is bought by creating a larger obligation.

scenarioShape.premise: A deniable courier run exposes the network that keeps frontier settlements alive.
scenarioShape.whyThisRun: The underground network keeps the pressure mobile and personal.
scenarioShape.selectionRationale: Black-channel pressure fits Driftrunner agency better than a paper audit.
scenarioShape.rejectedDefaultShape: A registry official demanding payment at a counter.

arcPlan.arcQuestion: Can the crew buy freedom without breaking the network that made freedom possible?
arcPlan.coreCrucible: The crew's exit depends on invisible infrastructure that exposure would destroy.

latentArcQuestions.question: What did the broker promise the Rogue AI cutout?
latentArcQuestions.whyItMatters: If the claim transfers, route freedom was never free.
latentArcQuestions.answerImpactAxis: Obligation and freedom: whose debt the crew actually carries, and what repayment means.

chapterFunctionMap.function: Establish the lien and courier offer.
chapterFunctionMap.pressureQuestion: What will the crew carry to leave?
Who pays when the trace starts?

errorIncludesNone: latent_arc_questions[0].answer_impact_axis must describe an impact axis, not a hidden answer
